description: Initialize claude-faces repo and top-level folder layout
steps: Run git init in the claude-faces repo root and set the default branch to main.
Create the top-level directory skeleton: app/, components/, lib/, public/, docs/, skill/agent-face/{references,scripts,assets}, and .github/workflows/.
Add .gitignore covering node_modules, .next/, out/, .env, .env*.local, .vercel/, coverage/, public/models/, .DS_Store, *.tsbuildinfo.
Add LICENSE (MIT, current year, Felipe Cavalcanti) since the skill frontmatter declares license: MIT.
Add .nvmrc pinning Node 22 (Next.js 16 requirement) and .editorconfig (2-space indent, LF, final newline).
Add a placeholder prd.json at repo root ([]) — this is the project deliverable, finalized by the last task.
Choose npm as the canonical package manager for maximum cross-harness portability; record the choice in docs/decisions.md.
Add a minimal README.md stub (title + one-line description) to be expanded by the README task.
Verify: ls shows app/ components/ lib/ public/ skill/ docs/ .github/ and prd.json, LICENSE, .gitignore present.
Verify: git status runs cleanly and node -e require('fs').readFileSync('prd.json') parses prd.json as a valid JSON array.

description: Scaffold Next.js 16 + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 base config
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Initialize claude-faces repo and top-level folder layout'.
Create package.json named claude-faces (private) with scripts: dev (next dev), build (next build), start (next start), lint (eslint .), typecheck (tsc --noEmit).
Add dependencies: next@16, react@^19, react-dom@^19, three@^0.185, @react-three/fiber@^9, @react-three/drei@^10, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, lucide-react.
Add devDependencies: typescript, @types/node, @types/react, @types/react-dom, @types/three, tailwindcss@^4, @tailwindcss/postcss, postcss, eslint, eslint-config-next.
Create tsconfig.json (paths alias @/*, strict, bundler moduleResolution, jsx react-jsx) and next-env.d.ts.
Create next.config.mjs (images.unoptimized true; COOP/COEP headers added by a later task) and postcss.config.mjs (@tailwindcss/postcss).
Create app/globals.css (Tailwind v4 @import 'tailwindcss' + dark theme tokens) and app/layout.tsx with neutral metadata (title 'Agent Face').
Create vercel.json (framework nextjs; per-function maxDuration for app/api/chat, app/api/transcribe, app/api/tts) so deploy config exists before the routes.
Run npm install and commit the generated package-lock.json.
Verify: npm run typecheck passes with no errors.
Verify: npm run build completes and produces a .next/ build.

description: Port the EIDOLON particle face component and neutralize branding
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Scaffold Next.js 16 + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 base config'.

Copy face-points.ts from reference/fugu-face/ (vendored EIDOLON source; original at ../fuguFaces on Felipe's machine — never modify it) into lib/face-points.ts: 12 emotions, COUNTS head/eye/mouth/brow, RANGES, EMOTION_META, buildTargets, TOTAL (~4,700 particles).
Copy agent-face.tsx (R3F Canvas + ParticleFace + Dust + OrbitControls), face-hud.tsx, and utils.ts from reference/fugu-face/ into components/agent-face.tsx, components/face-hud.tsx, and lib/utils.ts.
In face-hud.tsx, rename the hardcoded 'EIDOLON-01 / SPIKE v0.1' HUD label to a neutral configurable title (default 'AGENT FACE'); keep the 1-0/Q/W emotion keyboard shortcuts.
grep the copied files for hermes/Hermes/EIDOLON and genericize residual references.
Confirm the 12 emotions still enumerate: neutral, thinking, speaking, happy, alert, sad, angry, surprised, confused, sleepy, love, glitch.
Verify: npm run typecheck passes after the copy.
Verify: temporarily mount AgentFace with emotion='neutral', run npm run dev, and confirm the particle face renders and all 12 emotions cycle via keys 1-0/Q/W with no console errors.

description: Rebrand public assets, favicon, and layout metadata
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Scaffold Next.js 16 + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 base config'.
Add neutral icons in public/ (icon.svg, icon-light-32x32.png, icon-dark-32x32.png, apple-icon.png) — no Claude/Anthropic/Hermes marks.
Add public/og.png (1200x630) social preview placeholder and public/screenshots/{hero.png,talking.png} placeholders referenced by the README.
Update app/layout.tsx metadata: title 'Agent Face', a description of a talking, lip-syncing agent face, and icon wiring to the new assets.
Ensure no asset filename or alt text contains 'claude', 'anthropic', or 'hermes'.
Verify: npm run build succeeds and favicon/OG references resolve (no 404s in npm run dev).
Verify: grep -ri for claude/anthropic/hermes over public/ and app/layout.tsx returns no branding hits.

description: Define the provider env contract and .env.example with graceful degradation
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Scaffold Next.js 16 + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 base config'.
Create .env.example. Mode A chat keys (all optional): ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_MODEL, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL, GROQ_API_KEY, GROQ_DEFAULT_MODEL, OPENAI_API_KEY.

Mode B agent-bridge (optional): AGENT_BRIDGE_KIND (hermes|openclaw|claude-code|ollama|openai-compatible), AGENT_BRIDGE_URL, AGENT_BRIDGE_KEY, AGENT_BRIDGE_MODEL; plus Hermes-compatible HERMES_API_BASE_URL and HERMES_API_KEY.
STT + deploy knobs: OPENAI_TRANSCRIBE_MODEL (default whisper-1), OPENAI_TRANSCRIBE_LANGUAGE, OPENAI_TRANSCRIBE_PROMPT, SELF_HOST, ALLOW_AGENT_BRIDGE_IN_PROD.

Document in comments that every key is optional and the app degrades gracefully: no chat brain -> friendly 'configure a brain' message; no OPENAI/GROQ key -> browser Whisper STT + Web Speech TTS; agent-bridge appears only when its endpoint is reachable.

Write docs/env-contract.md specifying the chat-brain selection priority (explicit user pick, else ANTHROPIC -> OPENROUTER -> GROQ -> agent-bridge) and the exact env names so check-env.mjs and backends.md stay consistent.
State the security rule: all keys are server-side only; never NEXT_PUBLIC_*.
Verify: grep NEXT_PUBLIC .env.example returns nothing (no secret is client-exposed).
Verify: .env.example lists all Mode A + Mode B keys plus the transcription knobs and a degradation note.

description: 🚨 URGENT: Add cross-origin isolation headers so browser Whisper (transformers.js/WebGPU) can run
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Scaffold Next.js 16 + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 base config'.

Edit next.config.mjs to add an async headers() returning Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin and Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp (or credentialless) for all routes, enabling SharedArrayBuffer for WASM threads.
Add Permissions-Policy: microphone=(self) so getUserMedia is not blocked.
Self-host model files under public/models or set Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy so the Hugging Face CDN fetch does not fail under COEP; switch to credentialless if an embed breaks.
Confirm Vercel Analytics, OrbitControls, and fonts still load under COEP.
Add a comment marking these headers as load-bearing for the browser-Whisper path.
Verify: in devtools console self.crossOriginIsolated is true on the app origin.
Verify: navigator.gpu resolves an adapter in Chrome/Edge without a COEP error.
Verify: the particle face still renders with the new headers applied.

description: Build the shared Web Audio graph and wawa-lipsync amplitude/viseme engine
steps: Add dependency wawa-lipsync; run npm install.
Create lib/audio/context.ts exporting a lazily-created singleton AudioContext plus resumeAudio() that must be called from a user gesture (autoplay policy).

Create lib/lipsync.ts wrapping wawa-lipsync: createLipsync({fftSize, historySize}); connectMediaElement(el), connectStream(stream), processFrame() (call each rAF), getFeatures() returning {volume 0..1, viseme, visemeScores}.
Route all TTS/audio through the single AudioContext so the AnalyserNode taps exactly the audible audio (source -> analyser -> destination).
Add attack/decay smoothing on volume, clamp to 0..1, and add disconnect()/reset() to release the source node between utterances.
Add getEstimatedFeatures(text, elapsedMs) fallback for the Web Speech path (no tappable audio node) that synthesizes a plausible volume envelope from boundary events.
Verify: feeding a test audio element playing a spoken clip, getFeatures().volume oscillates 0..1 in sync with speech and returns non-empty viseme labels.
Verify: with no audio playing, getFeatures().volume decays to ~0.

description: Replace the fake sine-wave mouth with real audio-driven visemes in the particle renderer
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Build the shared Web Audio graph and wawa-lipsync amplitude/viseme engine' and 'Port the EIDOLON particle face component and neutralize branding'.
In components/agent-face.tsx add a mouthRef (useRef of {open, viseme}) passed into ParticleFace; remove the hardcoded mouthPulse = sin(...) expression (~line 85).
In the useFrame loop, read lip-sync features and compute mouthOpen 0..1.
Map amplitude to the mouth particle range RANGES.mouth (scale Y about MOUTH_Y for open) and widen X per current viseme (viseme_O narrows, viseme_aa widens), replacing the single mouthPulse factor.
Apply the audio-driven mouth only when emotion is 'speaking' or a speaking flag is set; otherwise keep the emotion's static mouth target.
Keep updates ref-based (no React state) so the 60fps loop over ~4,700 particles never triggers re-renders.
Expose an AgentFace prop mouthSource: 'analyser' | 'estimated' | 'off' so the orchestrator can pick the lip-sync path per TTS backend.
Verify: playing a TTS clip through the analyser visibly opens/closes the mouth particles in time with the audio, not on a fixed sine.
Verify: when audio stops, the mouth returns to the neutral/emotion target within one morph interval.
Verify: framerate stays ~60fps in Chrome devtools performance while lip-syncing.

description: Introduce a face-skin abstraction so EIDOLON and a TalkingHead/RPM skin are swappable
steps: Create lib/face/skin.ts defining interface FaceSkin { setEmotion(e); setSpeaking(on); setMouth(open, viseme?); setViseme(scores); mount(container); dispose() }.
Wrap the existing R3F renderer as components/skins/eidolon-skin.tsx implementing FaceSkin by driving the mouthRef/emotionRef.
Create components/face-skin.tsx that selects a skin by id ('eidolon' | 'talkinghead') from settings and renders it, defaulting to eidolon.

Add a stub components/skins/talkinghead-skin.tsx that lazy-loads TalkingHead.js + a Ready Player Me GLB, mapping Emotion to morph targets and viseme scores to ARKit/Oculus blendshapes; guard behind a flag and mark as stretch (must not block MVP).
Define a shared viseme vocabulary in lib/face/visemes.ts so wawa-lipsync output maps consistently onto either skin.
Route all emotion/mouth updates from the orchestrator through the FaceSkin interface rather than the concrete component.
Verify: switching skin id in settings swaps EIDOLON and the TalkingHead stub without touching orchestrator code.
Verify: selecting the talkinghead skin without the model present degrades to EIDOLON with a console warning, not a crash.

description: Implement the conversation-lifecycle emotion state machine
steps: Create lib/face/emotion-machine.ts exporting a pure reducer nextEmotion(phase, signals) mapping lifecycle to Emotion: idle->neutral, listening->alert, transcribing/waiting->thinking, streaming/speaking->speaking, clarifying->confused, error->glitch, success->happy, failure->sad.

Support model directive overrides: parse a trailing [[face:<emotion>]] token (match 'face' or 'emotion', a colon, and one lowercase emotion word inside double square brackets) as the resting emotion after speaking.
Add keyword-sentiment fallback (error/warning/clarify/success -> sad/alert/confused/happy) when no directive is present (port emotionFromReply).
Add hold/decay timing so transient states (surprised/happy) settle back to neutral after N ms.
Expose a useEmotion() hook or store that the orchestrator updates and FaceSkin subscribes to.
Ensure blink-suppression emotions (happy/sleepy/love/glitch) still animate correctly.
Verify: a unit test asserts each lifecycle phase yields the documented Emotion.
Verify: a reply ending in [[face:happy]] leaves the face happy after speaking, with the directive text stripped from spoken output.

description: Define the pluggable ChatAdapter interface, registry, and SSE helper
steps: Create lib/providers/types.ts defining ChatMessage {role, content}, ChatRequest {model, messages, system, temperature, signal}, and StreamEvent (delta | done | error).
Define interface ChatAdapter { id; label; listModels(); streamChat(req): AsyncIterable<StreamEvent>; available(env) } — the ONE seam that covers both brain modes (fresh API and agent-bridge).
Create lib/providers/index.ts as a registry mapping id to factory, plus resolveAdapter(id) and listAvailableAdapters(env) reflecting which server keys / reachable agents are present.
Add a shared SSE helper lib/providers/sse.ts using eventsource-parser to parse upstream token streams into StreamEvent; add dependency eventsource-parser.
Standardize error normalization so every adapter throws a typed AdapterError the /api/chat route maps to a status code.
Document the seam: adding a provider or wiring a new agent = one file implementing ChatAdapter + a registry entry.
Verify: npm run typecheck passes with a no-op FakeAdapter registered.
Verify: listAvailableAdapters() returns only adapters whose required env keys / reachable endpoints are present.

description: Implement the Anthropic Messages streaming adapter (Mode A)
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Define the pluggable ChatAdapter interface, registry, and SSE helper'.
Add dependency @anthropic-ai/sdk; run npm install.
Create lib/providers/anthropic.ts implementing ChatAdapter over the Messages API with stream:true (client.messages.stream).
Map ChatRequest.system to the top-level system param and messages to content blocks; translate content_block_delta events into StreamEvent deltas.
Read ANTHROPIC_API_KEY server-side only; available(env) = Boolean(ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
listModels() returns a curated set of current claude-* ids with a default overridable via ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_MODEL.
Honor req.signal so an aborted client (barge-in) cancels the upstream stream promptly.
Verify: a scripted streamChat over ANTHROPIC_API_KEY yields multiple delta events then a done event.
Verify: aborting the AbortSignal stops further deltas; a missing key excludes the adapter from the registry.

description: Implement the OpenRouter unified adapter (Mode A; also fronts Nous Hermes and other models)
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Define the pluggable ChatAdapter interface, registry, and SSE helper'.
Create lib/providers/openrouter.ts against the OpenAI-compatible endpoint https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions with stream:true.
Send OPENROUTER_API_KEY as Bearer plus HTTP-Referer and X-Title headers per OpenRouter etiquette.
Parse SSE data: chunks via lib/providers/sse.ts, extracting choices[0].delta.content into StreamEvent deltas and handling the [DONE] sentinel.
Implement listModels() by fetching https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models (brief cache) and pin known Nous Research Hermes model ids as favorites; default via OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL.
Map the system prompt into the messages array as a system role (OpenAI shape).
Honor req.signal for abort/barge-in.
Verify: streamChat against a cheap OpenRouter model yields incremental deltas then done.
Verify: listModels() includes at least one Hermes model; a 401 from a bad key surfaces as a typed AdapterError.

description: Implement the Groq fast-inference adapter (Mode A)
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Define the pluggable ChatAdapter interface, registry, and SSE helper'.
Create lib/providers/groq.ts against Groq's OpenAI-compatible endpoint https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions with stream:true.
Send GROQ_API_KEY as Bearer; parse SSE deltas via lib/providers/sse.ts.
Implement listModels() returning Groq's current fast models (e.g. llama-3.x family) with a default via GROQ_DEFAULT_MODEL; emphasize very-low-latency inference.
Map the system prompt into the messages array; honor req.signal.
available(env) = Boolean(GROQ_API_KEY) (the same key also enables hosted STT via whisper-large-v3-turbo).
Verify: streamChat yields deltas with a sub-second first token then done.
Verify: a missing GROQ_API_KEY excludes the adapter; a bad key surfaces as a typed AdapterError.

description: Implement the agent-bridge adapter (Mode B — reuse a running agent as the brain)
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Define the pluggable ChatAdapter interface, registry, and SSE helper'.
Create lib/providers/agent-bridge.ts implementing ChatAdapter for an ALREADY-RUNNING agent, kind selected by AGENT_BRIDGE_KIND:

- hermes: Hermes api_server session flow (ensureSession via POST /api/sessions, then POST /api/sessions/{id}/chat with X-Hermes-Session-Key; auth via HERMES_API_BASE_URL/HERMES_API_KEY or AGENT_BRIDGE_URL/KEY).
- ollama: POST {url}/api/chat with stream:true (NDJSON lines); models via {url}/api/tags.
- openclaw / claude-code / openai-compatible: POST {url}/v1/chat/completions (or the bridge's chat endpoint) parsed as SSE.
Reuse the agent's own memory/tools/persona: pass a stable session/thread id and do NOT resend full history when the agent is stateful.

available(env): a reachable endpoint is usable in localhost dev and self-host/VPS; on Vercel only when ALLOW_AGENT_BRIDGE_IN_PROD=1 or AGENT_BRIDGE_URL is a public HTTPS/tunnel (a serverless function cannot reach a private localhost).
Parse NDJSON/SSE into StreamEvent deltas robustly (message.content / choices[].delta.content / response); honor req.signal.
Return a clear 'agent offline' AdapterError when the endpoint is unreachable.
Verify: with a local Ollama running, streamChat yields deltas from a local model.
Verify: with AGENT_BRIDGE_KIND=hermes against a reachable Hermes api_server, a session is created and chat returns a reply.
Verify: in production with a private (non-public) AGENT_BRIDGE_URL, the adapter is absent from listAvailableAdapters().

description: Build the streaming /api/chat route that selects an adapter and streams tokens
steps: DEPENDS ON: the Anthropic, OpenRouter, Groq, and agent-bridge adapters and the registry.
Create app/api/chat/route.ts with runtime='nodejs' and a high maxDuration (model I/O wait is not billed CPU on Vercel).
Accept POST JSON { provider, model, messages, system }; reject bodies approaching the 4.5 MB request cap early.
Resolve the adapter via resolveAdapter(provider); return 400 with a machine-readable code if unknown or unavailable (missing key / unreachable agent).
Return a ReadableStream (text/event-stream) pumping adapter StreamEvents as SSE; flush per delta; set Cache-Control: no-cache and X-Accel-Buffering: no to defeat proxy buffering.
Wire request.signal into ChatRequest.signal so client disconnect/barge-in aborts the upstream call.
Never echo API keys; keep all provider secrets server-side.
Emit a terminal done event and, on failure, a safe error event.
Verify: curl --no-buffer streaming POST returns incrementally flushed SSE deltas, not one buffered blob.
Verify: aborting the client mid-stream stops upstream token generation.
Verify: an unknown provider returns 400 and a keyless/unreachable brain returns a clear 'provider unavailable' error.

description: Add /api/config capability probe so the UI knows which brains/STT/TTS are available
steps: Create app/api/config/route.ts (GET) returning booleans only: which Mode A adapters are available (anthropic/openrouter/groq), whether the Mode B agent-bridge is reachable/permitted, whether hosted STT keys exist (GROQ_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY), and whether hosted TTS (OPENAI_API_KEY) exists.
Derive availability from listAvailableAdapters(process.env) plus simple key-presence checks; never return key values or partial secrets.
Include the default model per available provider so the picker can preselect.
Add a short cache header; this endpoint has no secrets and is cheap.
Verify: GET /api/config returns { providers:{...}, agentBridge, stt:{...}, tts:{...} } reflecting the current env with no secret material.
Verify: removing a key flips the corresponding boolean; the response contains no 'sk-' or key-like substrings.

description: Build the streaming chat client that consumes /api/chat and drives thinking/speaking
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Build the streaming /api/chat route that selects an adapter and streams tokens'.

Create lib/chat/client.ts exporting async function* streamChat({provider, model, messages, system, signal}) that POSTs to /api/chat and yields token deltas parsed from the SSE stream (eventsource-parser in the browser).
Fire lifecycle callbacks: onStart -> emotion 'thinking', onFirstToken -> begin the 'speaking' handoff to TTS, onDone, onError -> 'glitch'.
Accumulate the assistant text and strip any trailing [[face:x]] directive before sending to TTS (delegate to the emotion machine).
Expose an AbortController so the UI can cancel (barge-in) mid-stream.
Buffer tokens into sentence-sized chunks and hand them to the TTS layer incrementally so speech can start before the full reply arrives.
Verify: calling streamChat renders tokens progressively in the transcript panel.
Verify: the first sentence is dispatched to TTS before the stream completes; aborting stops token accumulation and any queued TTS.

description: Add the conversation state store, system prompt, and persona
steps: Create lib/conversation.ts holding turns (user/assistant/system), the current provider/model, and the active persona/system prompt (prefer zero-dep useState + context for the MVP).
Define lib/persona.ts with a default FACE persona system prompt instructing concise spoken answers and optional [[face:<emotion>]] directives, generalized beyond Hermes.
Persist turns and settings to localStorage under a versioned key so a refresh restores the session; include a reset action.
Cap retained history length to keep /api/chat bodies well under the 4.5 MB request cap.
Expose addUserTurn, appendAssistantDelta, and finalizeAssistantTurn used by the chat client.
Verify: sending messages accumulates a coherent multi-turn history passed to /api/chat.
Verify: reloading restores prior turns and the selected provider/model; reset clears history and returns the face to neutral.

description: Implement microphone capture and push-to-talk recording
steps: Create lib/audio/recorder.ts wrapping getUserMedia({audio:true}) + MediaRecorder with opus-first MIME negotiation (audio/webm;codecs=opus preferred, mp4/ogg/wav fallbacks).
Expose start(), stop() returning a Blob, and a live MediaStream getter (needed for VAD and any stream-based lip-sync).
Request the mic only on the first record gesture; surface permission errors as recoverable UI state, not a crash.
Enforce a max clip length/size guard to stay well under the 4.5 MB hosted-STT request cap; auto-stop long recordings.
Resume the shared AudioContext on the record gesture (autoplay policy) so downstream lip-sync is unlocked.
Provide a push-to-talk binding (hold Space / press-and-hold the Talk button) that starts on press and stops on release.
Verify: hold-to-talk records and yields a non-empty opus blob under the size cap.
Verify: denying mic permission shows a typed hint and leaves typing available; releasing reliably ends the recording.

description: Add hands-free voice activity detection (VAD) with Silero/ONNX
steps: Add dependency @ricky0123/vad-web (and its onnxruntime-web peer); run npm install.
Create lib/audio/vad.ts wrapping MicVAD.new({ onSpeechStart, onSpeechEnd, positiveSpeechThreshold, minSpeechFrames }) on the shared MediaStream/AudioContext.
On onSpeechStart set lifecycle 'listening' (emotion alert); on onSpeechEnd hand the captured segment to the STT layer.
Ensure the Silero ONNX model + worklet assets resolve under the COEP headers (self-host under public/ or configure the asset base path).
Add a barge-in hook: if VAD detects speech while the face is speaking, cancel TTS and the in-flight chat.
Make VAD toggleable in settings and mutually exclusive with push-to-talk; debounce short noises.
Verify: hands-free, speaking a sentence auto-starts and auto-ends a turn end-to-end.
Verify: speaking over the agent's TTS interrupts it (barge-in); background noise below threshold does not start a turn.

description: Implement the in-browser Whisper worker (transformers.js, WebGPU with WASM fallback)
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Add cross-origin isolation headers so browser Whisper can run' (COOP/COEP).
Add dependency @huggingface/transformers; run npm install.

Create lib/stt/whisper-worker.ts as a Web Worker that lazy-imports pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', modelId) with device:'webgpu' and falls back to device:'wasm' on failure; modelId configurable (default Xenova/whisper-base).
Post messages for load progress, ready, transcribe (audio Float32Array/blob), and result; decode incoming webm/opus to PCM via the AudioContext before feeding the model.
Detect WebGPU via navigator.gpu, pick the backend, and report the chosen backend back to the UI.
Keep the pipeline resident between calls; handle worker errors/OOM by signaling a fallback-to-hosted.
Verify: the worker loads whisper-base once, reports progress, and transcribes a short clip to correct text offline (airplane mode).
Verify: on a machine without WebGPU it transparently uses WASM and still returns a transcript; a second transcription reuses the resident pipeline with no re-download.

description: Add the Whisper model download and caching UX
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Implement the in-browser Whisper worker'.
Create components/stt-status.tsx showing model download progress (worker progress events), cached/ready state, and the active backend (WebGPU/WASM).
Persist that the model is cached (transformers.js uses the browser Cache Storage) and show 'ready, offline-capable' once loaded.
Offer a 'download model' affordance on first browser-STT use so the ~150 MB download is a deliberate, explained action; show estimated size and a cancel/skip-to-hosted option.
Surface backend + model in the HUD so users understand $0/private mode is active.
Verify: first run shows a progress bar to 100% then 'ready'.
Verify: a reload skips re-download and shows 'cached/ready'; canceling routes STT to the hosted fallback.

description: Upgrade /api/transcribe into a hosted STT fallback (Groq whisper-large-v3-turbo, OpenAI fallback)
steps: Create app/api/transcribe/route.ts to accept a multipart audio field named 'audio' and select a provider: Groq whisper-large-v3-turbo when GROQ_API_KEY is set (cheapest/fastest), else OpenAI gpt-4o-transcribe/whisper-1 when OPENAI_API_KEY is set.
Keep runtime='nodejs'; enforce a byte cap comfortably under the 4.5 MB request limit and reject oversize clips with 413.
Forward to Groq's OpenAI-compatible https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/audio/transcriptions or OpenAI /v1/audio/transcriptions with model + optional language + prompt (vocab hints).
Return { text, provider, model, latencyMs }; normalize upstream errors to safe JSON with correct status codes.
Keep both GROQ_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY server-side; add a language/prompt passthrough from settings.
Verify: with GROQ_API_KEY set, a short webm/opus clip returns accurate text and provider 'groq'.
Verify: with only OPENAI_API_KEY set, it falls back to OpenAI; an oversize clip returns 413 with no upstream call.

description: Implement STT auto-selection (browser-first, hosted fallback)
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Implement the in-browser Whisper worker' and 'Upgrade /api/transcribe into a hosted STT fallback'.
Create lib/stt/index.ts exporting transcribe(blob, {mode}) where mode is 'browser' | 'hosted' | 'auto'.

In 'auto', prefer the browser worker when WebGPU/WASM is usable and the model is cached; on worker error, timeout, or missing model, POST to /api/transcribe (confirm a hosted key exists via /api/config).
If no hosted key and browser STT is unavailable, return a typed 'no STT available' error the UI can explain.
Record which engine served the transcript for the HUD; wire this as the single entry point for push-to-talk and VAD.
Verify: 'auto' uses the browser worker when cached and hosted when it is not.
Verify: pulling the network still transcribes via the cached browser model; with neither path, the UI shows a clear message and keeps text input working.

description: Implement Web Speech API TTS with chunking and boundary-driven mouth
steps: Create lib/tts/web-speech.ts wrapping SpeechSynthesis: pick a voice, expose speak(text), cancel(), and lifecycle callbacks (onStart/onEnd/onBoundary).
Chunk text into <=~200-char / sentence-sized utterances to dodge the ~15s Chrome truncation bug, queueing sequentially.
NOTE: SpeechSynthesis exposes no tappable audio node, so drive the mouth from onboundary word events + a synthetic envelope (lib/lipsync getEstimatedFeatures) rather than the FFT analyser.
Set emotion 'speaking' on the first utterance start and return to the resting emotion on the final utterance end.
Handle voiceschanged to populate available voices; expose them to settings; ensure cancel() fully stops the queue for barge-in.
Verify: a multi-sentence reply speaks fully without the ~15s cutoff.
Verify: the mouth animates during speech via the estimated envelope and settles when done; cancel() immediately silences queued utterances.

description: Add the streaming gpt-4o-mini-tts route for high-quality voice-out
steps: Create app/api/tts/route.ts (runtime='nodejs') accepting POST { text, voice, format } and calling OpenAI /v1/audio/speech with model gpt-4o-mini-tts, streaming the audio bytes back to the client.
Return a streamed Response with the correct Content-Type (audio/mpeg) and no proxy buffering so playback can start before the full clip arrives.
Keep OPENAI_API_KEY server-side; validate text length and reject empty/oversized input.
Prefer a chunk-per-sentence contract so the orchestrator can request TTS incrementally as chat tokens arrive.
Return a clear error if no key so the client falls back to Web Speech; honor request.signal so barge-in aborts synthesis.
Verify: POST returns a playable streamed audio response that starts before completion.
Verify: the returned audio, played through an audio element on the shared AudioContext, is tappable by the wawa-lipsync analyser; a missing key returns a typed error the client handles.

description: Build the TTS router that routes real audio into the lip-sync analyser and supports barge-in
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Add the streaming gpt-4o-mini-tts route', 'Build the shared Web Audio graph and wawa-lipsync amplitude/viseme engine', and 'Implement Web Speech API TTS with chunking and boundary-driven mouth'.
Create lib/tts/index.ts exposing speak(text, {engine}) where engine is 'web-speech' | 'openai' | 'kokoro' and a global stop() for barge-in.

For 'openai', fetch /api/tts as a stream, feed it into an audio element created on the shared AudioContext, and connect it through lib/lipsync connectMediaElement so the mouth is driven by the REAL FFT analyser (mouthSource 'analyser').
For 'web-speech', use the estimated-envelope path (mouthSource 'estimated').
Drive the emotion machine: 'speaking' while audio plays, resting emotion on ended.
Implement stop(): cancel the Web Speech queue, pause+reset the audio element, abort the /api/tts fetch, and disconnect the analyser source so a new utterance attaches cleanly.
Queue sentence chunks from the chat client so speech starts on the first sentence.
Verify: OpenAI TTS produces real FFT-driven lip-sync visibly better than the Web Speech estimate.
Verify: barge-in silences audio and stops the mouth within a frame; switching engine changes voice + mouthSource with no leftover analyser connections.

description: Add the all-local Kokoro-82M WebGPU TTS engine (stretch)
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Build the TTS router' and the lip-sync analyser.

Add dependency kokoro-js; create lib/tts/kokoro.ts loading Kokoro-82M on WebGPU (WASM fallback) in a worker, exposing speak(text) returning audio playable through an audio element/AudioBuffer on the shared AudioContext.
Route Kokoro output through lib/lipsync connectMediaElement so it uses the real FFT lip-sync path (mouthSource 'analyser'), same as OpenAI TTS.
Show a model download/cache UX consistent with the Whisper status component; gate behind a settings toggle; fall back to Web Speech on failure.
Keep this off the MVP critical path; it must not block the core loop.
Verify: with Kokoro selected, replies are spoken fully offline and drive FFT lip-sync.
Verify: a Kokoro load failure degrades to Web Speech without a crash; the model caches and reloads without re-download.

description: Build the settings panel with brain picker, keys hint, voice, face, and STT mode
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Add /api/config capability probe' and the provider registry.
Create components/settings-panel.tsx (drawer) reading /api/config to show only available brains and to disable unavailable ones with an explanation.

Add a brain picker: a Mode A provider (Anthropic/OpenRouter/Groq) + a model dropdown from the proxied listModels() with the server default preselected; or Mode B agent-bridge (shown when reachable) with its kind/URL fields in dev/self-host.
Add controls for STT mode (browser | hosted | auto), TTS engine (web-speech | openai | kokoro), face skin (eidolon | talkinghead), and push-to-talk vs VAD hands-free.
Persist all settings via lib/conversation.ts localStorage; changes take effect without reload.
Do NOT collect provider API keys in the browser — keys stay server-side; show a hint pointing to env config when a brain is unavailable.
Verify: only server-available brains are selectable; unavailable ones show why.
Verify: changing model/voice/skin/STT-mode updates behavior immediately and survives reload; agent-bridge controls are hidden when /api/config reports it unreachable.

description: Wire the full conversation orchestrator (listen -> transcribe -> chat -> speak -> lip-sync -> emotion)
steps: DEPENDS ON: recorder, VAD, STT auto-selection, chat client, TTS router, emotion machine, and the face-skin.

Create lib/orchestrator.ts (or a useConversation hook) that owns the lifecycle FSM and sequences: capture (PTT or VAD) -> transcribe(blob, mode) -> streamChat -> chunked speak -> lip-sync -> emotion transitions.
Rebuild app/page.tsx around the orchestrator + FaceSkin + FaceHud + SettingsPanel (replacing any earlier request/response + speechSynthesis placeholder).
Start TTS on the first streamed sentence (not after full completion) for low latency; keep the transcript panel updating live.
Drive FaceSkin.setEmotion/setSpeaking/setMouth from orchestrator events; strip [[face:x]] before speaking and apply it as the resting emotion.
Implement global interrupt (barge-in): a new user utterance or VAD-during-speech aborts the chat AbortController and calls TTS stop().
Keep heavy work off the React render path (refs/worker messages) to protect the 60fps particle loop.
Verify: a full spoken turn runs end-to-end: mic -> transcript -> streamed reply -> spoken audio -> lip-synced mouth -> resting emotion.
Verify: interrupting mid-reply cleanly starts a new turn; the face shows thinking during the stream and speaking synced to audio.

description: Implement graceful degradation when no server keys are present
steps: DEPENDS ON: '/api/config', STT auto-selection, TTS router, and the orchestrator.
On load, read /api/config and browser capabilities (navigator.gpu, MediaRecorder, SpeechSynthesis) to compute an available-features matrix.

With no keys, allow a fully-local experience where possible: browser-Whisper STT + Web Speech TTS + the agent-bridge in dev; if no chat brain is reachable, show a clear 'add a key or wire your running agent' state while keeping the face interactive.
Auto-select STT 'browser' and TTS 'web-speech' when hosted keys are absent; select hosted only when keys exist.
Never hard-fail the UI on a missing capability; each missing piece shows an explanatory, actionable message; typing-to-chat works whenever any brain is available.
Verify: with zero env keys the app boots, the face animates, and browser STT + Web Speech still function for a local/dev brain.
Verify: removing GROQ/OPENAI keys routes STT to the browser worker and TTS to Web Speech; with no brain at all, the UI explains what to configure instead of throwing.

description: Create the skill/agent-face package and SKILL.md thin router
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Initialize claude-faces repo and top-level folder layout'.
Create the portable skill directory skill/agent-face/ with subfolders references/, scripts/, and assets/.
Author skill/agent-face/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter: name: agent-face (MUST NOT contain 'claude' or 'anthropic' — reserved), description, version, license: MIT, and neutral metadata.

Write the description to state WHAT (gives an AI agent a talking, animated, lip-syncing voice face, deployable on Vercel or self-hosted), WHEN to use it, and trigger phrases ('give my agent a face', 'talk to my agent by voice', 'deploy a face UI', 'lip-sync face', 'put a face on Hermes/openclaw').
Keep SKILL.md a THIN ROUTER under 500 lines: short Overview, When-to-Use, a numbered operating flow, and links to references/*.md (progressive disclosure).
Add a Portability section stating the skill uses only harness-agnostic node/bash scripts and works on Claude Code, Hermes, openclaw, trustclaw, nanoclaw.
Describe the two brain modes (A fresh API, B bring-your-own running agent) and point at references/backends.md.
Verify: wc -l skill/agent-face/SKILL.md is <= 500.
Verify: a grep for a frontmatter name containing claude/anthropic returns nothing, and the description contains explicit trigger phrases plus a WHEN clause.

description: Add self-host artifacts (Dockerfile + compose) for hosting next to the agent
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Wire the full conversation orchestrator' (buildable app) and the app/api/* routes.
Create a multi-stage Dockerfile (install -> next build -> next start on PORT) and set Next output: 'standalone' for a slim runtime image.
Create docker-compose.yml running the app plus an AGENT_BRIDGE_URL env pointing at the agent's private network address, with all env vars passed via compose/.env.
Add a self-host section (in references/deploy.md) describing running on the agent's own VPS so Mode B reaches the agent over the private network with no tunnel.
Verify: docker compose up serves the app on the configured port.
Verify: with an agent reachable on the same network, Mode B (agent-bridge) connects over its private URL without a tunnel.

description: Package the app template into skill assets with a drift-parity check
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Create the skill/agent-face package and SKILL.md thin router' and a building app (typecheck/build pass).

Create skill/agent-face/assets/app-template/ as a self-contained copy of the deployable app: app/, components/, lib/, public/, package.json, package-lock.json, tsconfig.json, next.config.mjs, postcss.config.mjs, vercel.json, next-env.d.ts, Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and a template .env.example.
Add skill/agent-face/assets/app-template/README.md pointing back to the skill references.

Set the single-source-of-truth policy: the repo-root app is canonical and the template is a synced snapshot; add skill/agent-face/scripts/sync-template.mjs that regenerates the template from the repo root and fails on divergence (--check).
Add a .gitignore inside the template so scaffolded copies ignore node_modules/.next/.env*.local; exclude node_modules/.next/.vercel from the packaged template.
Verify: node skill/agent-face/scripts/sync-template.mjs --check reports parity.
Verify: copy assets/app-template to a temp dir, run npm install && npm run build, and confirm it builds standalone.

description: Write the portable scaffold script
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Package the app template into skill assets with a drift-parity check'.

Create skill/agent-face/scripts/scaffold.mjs as a Node ESM script (no Claude-specific tools, no external deps) that copies assets/app-template/ into a target directory passed as the first arg (default ./agent-face-app).
Refuse to overwrite a non-empty target unless --force is passed; recursively copy while skipping node_modules/.next.
After copy, print next steps: cp .env.example .env.local, npm install, then how to run the dev script.
Support a --install flag (npm install in the target), a --help flag, and resolve the template path relative to the script so it works when the skill is extracted standalone.
Make it cross-platform (node:fs/node:path, avoid bash-only cp semantics).
Verify: node skill/agent-face/scripts/scaffold.mjs /tmp/af-test creates the app files in the target.
Verify: re-running without --force on a populated target exits non-zero with a clear message; cd /tmp/af-test && npm run build succeeds after install.

description: Write the dev-with-kill script that kills any previous server before starting
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Package the app template into skill assets with a drift-parity check'.

Create skill/agent-face/scripts/dev.mjs (Node ESM) that FIRST kills any previous dev server before starting a new one — this kill step is a HARD REQUIREMENT (matches the owner's standing 'always kill previous process' rule).

Implement the kill portably: find any process listening on the dev port (default 3000, overridable via --port) using lsof -ti tcp:PORT on macOS/Linux, and terminate stray next dev processes; SIGTERM then SIGKILL if still alive.
After the port is confirmed free, spawn npm run dev in the app dir (current dir if it has package.json, else assets/app-template).
Once the server is listening, open the browser (macOS open / Linux xdg-open / Windows start), skipped in headless/CI or with --no-open.
Stream child stdout/stderr and forward Ctrl-C to stop the child cleanly.
Verify: run the script twice in a row; only one process listens on the port (lsof -ti tcp:3000 | wc -l returns 1).
Verify: the browser opens to the running app (or is skipped with --no-open) and the face renders.

description: Write the check-env script
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Define the provider env contract and .env.example with graceful degradation'.
Create skill/agent-face/scripts/check-env.mjs (Node ESM) that reads the environment (and optionally a .env.local in the target app) and reports which brains are configured.
Report per capability: Mode A chat providers (ANTHROPIC/OPENROUTER/GROQ), Mode B agent-bridge reachability, STT (GROQ/OPENAI), TTS (OPENAI); print a clear check/cross table.
NEVER print secret values — only presence/absence (mask everything).
Print a resolved summary: which brain would be selected by the documented priority, and whether voice input will work.
Exit 0 if at least one chat path is usable, non-zero with guidance otherwise; support --json and --help.
Verify: with no keys set, the script prints all crosses, a 'no brain configured' hint, and exits non-zero.
Verify: with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set, it prints Anthropic available, names it as the selected brain, and exits 0 without leaking the value.

description: Write the deploy script with vercel and self-host targets
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Add self-host artifacts (Dockerfile + compose)' and the app vercel.json.
Create skill/agent-face/scripts/deploy.mjs (Node ESM) with --target vercel|self-host (default vercel), --prod/--preview, and --help.
Preflight (both targets): verify required app files exist (package.json, app/api/chat/route.ts, app/api/transcribe/route.ts, vercel.json) before doing anything.

vercel target: confirm the vercel CLI is installed (vercel --version; else print npm i -g vercel and exit non-zero); run vercel link (idempotent); warn (do not hard-fail) if no provider env is present; npm install; npm run typecheck; npm run build; vercel deploy --prod; print the URL + an HTTPS-mic reminder.
self-host target: run npm run build (or docker build) and print the runnable docker compose up (or next start) command plus how to set AGENT_BRIDGE_URL to the agent's private URL.
Verify: with vercel absent and --target vercel, the script exits non-zero with the install hint and no partial deploy.
Verify: --target self-host builds and prints a runnable docker compose / next start command; a missing app file fails preflight fast before any network call.

description: Wire the operating flow into SKILL.md (attach-to-host-agent, localhost-or-deploy, target pick)
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Write the dev-with-kill script', 'Write the deploy script with vercel and self-host targets', and 'Write the check-env script'.

In SKILL.md add an explicit operating sequence: (1) run check-env; (2) offer Mode B — if the host agent (Hermes/openclaw/Claude Code/Ollama) exposes a reachable endpoint, offer to wire the agent-bridge to it so the face reuses that agent as its brain, else fall back to a Mode A key; (3) ASK verbatim 'Run on localhost to try it first, or deploy?'; (4) if deploy, ASK 'Vercel (hosted) or self-host on your VPS?'.
Document the localhost branch: run node scripts/dev.mjs (kills any prior server first), open the browser, let the user talk to the face.
Document the deploy branch: node scripts/deploy.mjs --target <vercel|self-host> after confirming env with check-env.
Make clear the agent MUST wait for and act on each answer rather than assuming a default.

Reference each script by relative path and one-line purpose so any harness can invoke them with plain node; add an after-deploy verification snippet (open URL, press talk, confirm transcript + spoken reply + face morph).
Verify: SKILL.md contains the exact question strings and all branches with correct script paths.
Verify: SKILL.md remains under 500 lines after the additions.

description: Author the skill triggering evaluations
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Create the skill/agent-face package and SKILL.md thin router' and 'Wire the operating flow into SKILL.md'.
Create skill/agent-face/references/evaluations.md following Anthropic skill best practices with at least 3 evaluation scenarios (realistic prompt, expected flow, pass/fail criterion).
Add a 'Should trigger' list: 'give my agent a face', 'I want to talk to my agent and see it lip-sync', 'deploy a voice face UI to Vercel', 'put a face on my Hermes/openclaw agent'.

Add a 'Should NOT trigger' list: 'generate a headshot photo', 'build a face-recognition model', 'add face-detection to my camera app', 'write a cron job' — cases that mention 'face' but are unrelated.
Ensure the SKILL.md description's trigger phrases align with the Should-trigger cases (tune wording if a case would be missed).
Document how to run the evaluations manually against a harness and record pass/fail.
Verify: evaluations.md contains >= 3 scenarios plus both a Should-trigger and Should-NOT-trigger section.
Verify: each Should-trigger phrase is covered by a trigger phrase or keyword in SKILL.md's description.

description: Verify cross-harness portability and document the harness matrix
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Write the portable scaffold script', 'Write the dev-with-kill script', 'Write the check-env script', and 'Write the deploy script with vercel and self-host targets'.
Audit all scripts to confirm they use only Node/bash built-ins and no Claude-specific tools, MCP calls, or harness APIs.
Run a grep over skill/agent-face/scripts for anthropic|claude|mcp|allowed-tools|tool_use and confirm no harness-coupled references remain.
Confirm every script runs under a plain runtime: node skill/agent-face/scripts/<name>.mjs --help for scaffold/dev/check-env/deploy.

Write skill/agent-face/references/portability.md with a harness matrix (Claude Code, Hermes, openclaw, trustclaw, nanoclaw) and how each invokes the skill (SKILL.md discovery + running the node scripts).
Confirm SKILL.md frontmatter fields are the portable/open subset (name, description, version, license); any harness-specific keys live under metadata.
Verify: all four --help invocations succeed under node with no missing-dependency errors.
Verify: the portability grep returns zero harness-coupled matches in scripts.

description: Write references/architecture.md and references/backends.md
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Create the skill/agent-face package and SKILL.md thin router' and 'Define the provider env contract and .env.example with graceful degradation'.

Write skill/agent-face/references/architecture.md: the runtime flow (browser MediaRecorder -> STT browser/hosted -> /api/chat brain -> TTS + face morph), the deploy boundary (Vercel vs self-host), and the security model (server-side keys only).
Include a mermaid sequence diagram of the voice loop.
Document the repo layout (root Next.js app, app/api/* thin proxies, skill/ package, prd.json).

Write skill/agent-face/references/backends.md: Mode A (Anthropic/OpenRouter/Groq) + Mode B (agent-bridge kinds hermes/openclaw/claude-code/ollama/openai-compatible), their env vars, the selection priority, reachability rules, and graceful degradation.
Add 'How to wire your own agent' and 'How to add a provider' subsections (the single place the /api/chat route dispatches).
Verify: every env var named in backends.md matches .env.example exactly (diff the key names).
Verify: the architecture mermaid block renders (fenced mermaid) and contains no Hermes-only references.

description: Write references/face.md and references/voice.md
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Port the EIDOLON particle face component and neutralize branding'.

Write skill/agent-face/references/face.md: the 12 emotions, the [[face:<emotion>]] directive syntax the model can emit, how UI state maps to emotions, and how the particle geometry/morph works (reference lib/face-points.ts COUNTS and buildTargets).
Document how to customize colors/emotions by editing EMOTION_META and the per-emotion target builders; list the 1-0/Q/W keyboard shortcuts.

Write skill/agent-face/references/voice.md: STT (browser Whisper via transformers.js + hosted Groq/OpenAI fallback), TTS (Web Speech vs streamed gpt-4o-mini-tts vs Kokoro), the two lip-sync paths (real FFT analyser vs estimated envelope) and why Web Speech cannot be FFT-tapped, and mic/HTTPS requirements.
Verify: face.md lists exactly the 12 emotions present in lib/face-points.ts.
Verify: voice.md's env var names (OPENAI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY, OPENAI_TRANSCRIBE_*) match .env.example.

description: Write references/deploy.md and references/troubleshooting.md
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Write the deploy script with vercel and self-host targets' and 'Add self-host artifacts (Dockerfile + compose)'.

Write skill/agent-face/references/deploy.md: the localhost-first flow, both deploy targets (Vercel button + vercel CLI, and self-host Docker on a VPS), how to set the env vars, and Mode B reachability (private network vs an HTTPS tunnel from Vercel).

Write skill/agent-face/references/troubleshooting.md covering at least six categories: browser cannot record (HTTPS/MediaRecorder), transcription fails (keys/size/model), chat brain fails (which key / provider auth / rate limits / agent offline), face does not change emotion (invalid directive ignored), dev server port in use (dev.mjs kill), and cross-origin not isolated (browser Whisper) — each with a concrete check. Add a 'nothing configured' entry explaining the degradation messages.
Add a portability entry: running the scripts under a non-Claude harness (plain node scripts/*.mjs).
Verify: troubleshooting.md includes the six failure categories, each with a concrete check.
Verify: deploy.md commands cover both targets and reference no removed Hermes-only variable.

description: Write the human-facing README
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Wire the full conversation orchestrator' (page renders) and 'Rebrand public assets, favicon, and layout metadata'.

Rewrite root README.md outcomes-first: open with what you get (talk to your agent, hear a voice reply, watch an animated lip-syncing face) and a hero screenshot placeholder from public/screenshots/hero.png.
Add a Quick Start: git clone, npm install, cp .env.example .env.local, add a provider key OR point it at your running agent, npm run dev, open localhost, press talk.

Add a 'Two brains' section: Mode A (Anthropic/OpenRouter/Groq) vs Mode B (bring your own running agent: Hermes/openclaw/Claude Code/Ollama), with the north-star line 'install the skill on an agent and it gets a face'.
Add a 'Deploy' section (button added by the dedicated task) plus a self-host note; link to skill/agent-face/references/deploy.md.

Add an 'Install the skill' section explaining the portable skill/agent-face folder works on Claude Code, Hermes, openclaw, trustclaw, nanoclaw, and how to invoke the scaffold/dev/check-env/deploy scripts.
Add a brains table (Mode A providers + Mode B agent-bridge kinds), the optional env vars, and a screenshots section (public/screenshots/talking.png).
Verify: README renders with no broken relative links.
Verify: a grep for 'quick start', 'deploy', 'skill', and 'brain' confirms all major sections are present.

description: Add the Deploy with Vercel button and env prompt wiring
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Write the human-facing README' and 'Define the provider env contract and .env.example with graceful degradation'.

Construct the Deploy button URL: https://vercel.com/new/clone?repository-url=<REPO_URL>&env=ANTHROPIC_API_KEY,OPENROUTER_API_KEY,GROQ_API_KEY,OPENAI_API_KEY&envDescription=...&envLink=<REPO_URL>/blob/main/skill/agent-face/references/backends.md&project-name=claude-faces&repository-name=claude-faces.
Add the markdown button [Deploy with Vercel](clone-url) using https://vercel.com/button into the README Deploy section.
Set envDescription to make clear all four keys are optional and the app degrades gracefully; point envLink at backends.md.
Confirm the env list contains exactly the four Mode A keys (Mode B is configured post-deploy since it needs a reachable agent).
Document that the repository-url must be updated to the final public GitHub URL once the repo is pushed.
Verify: the button URL parses and its env query param equals ANTHROPIC_API_KEY,OPENROUTER_API_KEY,GROQ_API_KEY,OPENAI_API_KEY.
Verify: clicking the rendered button opens the Vercel clone page listing all four env prompts (manual check).

description: Write CONTRIBUTING.md and developer setup docs
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON 'Write the human-facing README'.

Write CONTRIBUTING.md: local setup (Node 22, npm install), the scripts, code style (typecheck/lint before PR), and the single-source-of-truth rule that app changes must be synced into skill/agent-face/assets/app-template via sync-template.mjs.
Document the branch/PR workflow and that CI must pass (typecheck, build, skill-lint, template parity, portability, test:skill).
Add docs/development.md describing the repo layout and the boundary between scaffolding and app-internals work.
Note how to run and test the four skill scripts locally under a plain node runtime.
Link CONTRIBUTING.md from the README.
Verify: CONTRIBUTING.md references the sync-template parity requirement and the CI checks by name.
Verify: relative links in CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/development.md resolve.

description: Set up the app test/eval harness (Vitest unit + Playwright browser)
steps: Add devDeps vitest, @vitest/ui, jsdom, @playwright/test; run npm install and npx playwright install chromium.
Add scripts to package.json: test (vitest run), test:e2e (playwright test), and test:watch.
Create vitest.config.ts (jsdom env) and playwright.config.ts (baseURL http://localhost:3000, webServer npm run dev) that respect the COEP/COOP headers.
Add fixtures under tests/fixtures/: a short spoken webm/wav clip for STT smoke tests and a sample TTS audio clip for lip-sync tests.
Add a mock ChatAdapter and a fake SSE server helper so streaming tests do not hit real providers.
Grant Playwright a fake mic (--use-fake-device-for-media-stream / permissions context) for deterministic audio tests.
Verify: npm test runs an empty suite green; npm run test:e2e launches the dev server and loads the app.
Verify: the harness can grant mic permission and inject a fake audio stream.

description: Eval: STT accuracy smoke test (browser + hosted)
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Set up the app test/eval harness', 'Implement the in-browser Whisper worker', and 'Upgrade /api/transcribe into a hosted STT fallback'.
Create tests/stt.smoke.test.ts feeding a known fixture clip through lib/stt transcribe(blob, {mode:'browser'}) and asserting the transcript matches expected text within an edit-distance tolerance.
Add a hosted-path case (mode:'hosted') mocking /api/transcribe to return a fixed transcript to validate wiring without spending API credits.
Assert 'auto' falls back from browser to hosted when the worker throws; assert the served-engine label is reported correctly.
Keep the real-model browser assertion tolerant (whisper-base is imperfect) but require key content words present.
Verify: the browser-Whisper transcript contains the expected keywords; the auto fallback path is exercised.
Verify: the test runs without real network calls for the hosted case.

description: Eval: streaming chat works end-to-end through /api/chat
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Set up the app test/eval harness', the provider registry, and '/api/chat'.
Create tests/chat.stream.test.ts registering the mock ChatAdapter that emits several deltas then done, and asserting /api/chat returns incrementally flushed SSE events in order.
Assert the route returns 400 for an unknown provider and a typed 'unavailable' error for a keyless/unreachable brain.
Assert request abort propagates to adapter cancellation (spy on the AbortSignal).
Add a client test that lib/chat/client.ts streamChat yields progressive tokens and strips a trailing [[face:x]] directive; assert the first-sentence handoff to TTS fires before completion.
Verify: SSE deltas arrive incrementally, not as one buffered blob; abort cancels upstream and stops token accumulation.
Verify: unknown/keyless providers return the documented error codes.

description: Eval: lip-sync fires on real audio
steps: DEPENDS ON: 'Build the shared Web Audio graph and wawa-lipsync amplitude/viseme engine' and 'Build the TTS router'.
Create tests/lipsync.test.ts (Playwright) that plays the fixture TTS audio through an audio element on the shared AudioContext connected to lib/lipsync, sampling getFeatures().volume over time.
Assert volume rises above a threshold during speech and decays to ~0 during silence (envelope non-flat).
Assert the mouth particle displacement (exposed via a test hook on the EidolonSkin) correlates with volume, i.e. the mouth is not on a fixed sine when real audio plays.
Add a Web-Speech-path assertion that the estimated envelope drives non-zero mouth motion via onboundary events.
Guard for headless WebGPU/WebAudio availability and fall back to WASM/analyser as needed.
Verify: analyser volume is non-flat and time-aligned with the fixture audio; mouth displacement tracks volume.

description: Eval: emotion transitions across the conversation lifecycle

description: Keep server-only provider SDKs out of the client bundle
steps: CONTEXT (found 2026-07-18 while debugging the build): app/page.tsx is a 'use client' component and transitively imports the server-only provider chain — app/page.tsx -> lib/use-orchestrator.ts -> lib/orchestrator.ts -> lib/providers/index.ts -> lib/providers/anthropic.ts -> @anthropic-ai/sdk. This ships a ~151K client chunk containing the Anthropic SDK (anthropic-version / x-api-key / dangerouslyAllowBrowser strings appear in .next/static/chunks) to the browser, and makes `next build --webpack` fail outright with UnhandledSchemeError on node:path. No key material leaks today because Next only inlines NEXT_PUBLIC_*, but it violates the CLAUDE.md rule that provider SDKs and keys stay server-side.

RED first: add lib/providers/client-boundary.test.ts that statically walks the import graph reachable from lib/use-orchestrator.ts (read files, follow relative and @/ alias imports) and asserts no provider SDK is reachable — fail if '@anthropic-ai/sdk' or lib/providers/anthropic appears. Confirm this test FAILS before the fix.

Split the registry: keep lib/providers/index.ts server-only (it is what app/api/chat/route.ts uses) and move what the client legitimately needs — adapter ids, display names, capability flags, TypeScript types — into a client-safe module (e.g. lib/providers/catalog.ts) that imports NO SDK.

Repoint lib/orchestrator.ts, lib/use-orchestrator.ts and app/page.tsx at the client-safe module. The client learns about providers only via types plus the existing /api/config and /api/models routes; it must never construct an adapter.
Prefer the static import-graph assertion over adding a new 'server-only' dependency — the assertion is portable and needs no new package.
If any root app file changed, re-run: node skill/agent-face/scripts/sync-template.mjs
Verify: the new client-boundary test passes and npm test is fully green (no previously-passing test regresses).
Verify: npm run build exits 0 AND `grep -rlE 'anthropic-version|dangerouslyAllowBrowser' .next/static/chunks/` returns NO files.
Verify: npx next build --webpack completes without the 'Reading from "node:path" is not handled by plugins' UnhandledSchemeError.
Verify: npm run typecheck exits 0.

description: Fix the SSR/client hydration mismatch on the voice control button
steps: CONTEXT (found 2026-07-18 by the Playwright harness, task 'Set up the app test/eval harness'): loading / logs a React hydration mismatch at app/page.tsx:164. lib/capabilities.ts:77 computes `microphone: Boolean(nav?.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia)`, but there is no `navigator` during SSR, so the server renders 'VOICE UNAVAILABLE — TYPE BELOW' while the client renders 'HOLD TO TALK (OR SPACE)'. app/page.tsx reads the capability matrix directly during render with no mount guard. This is NOT dev-only noise: in production the user sees a flash of the wrong, discouraging label before React corrects it client-side.

RED first: add a test asserting the capability matrix is SSR-safe — evaluating it with no `navigator`/`window` must yield the same *rendered control label* the client settles on, or the component must render a stable placeholder until mounted. Confirm it FAILS first.

Fix by rendering a deterministic, capability-agnostic initial state on the server and resolving real capabilities after mount (useEffect/useSyncExternalStore with a server snapshot), so server and client HTML agree.
Do NOT paper over it with suppressHydrationWarning — that hides the mismatch instead of fixing it.
Keep the no-capability path honest: when voice truly is unavailable after mount, the button must still show the unavailable state and the type-to-chat fallback must work.
If any root app file changed, re-run: node skill/agent-face/scripts/sync-template.mjs
Verify: npm test green, npm run typecheck exits 0.
Verify: npm run test:e2e passes AND the dev-server output contains no 'hydration' / 'did not match' warning during the smoke run.

description: Guard the Eidolon skin against double createRoot on the same container
steps: CONTEXT (found 2026-07-18 by the Playwright harness): the browser console logs 'You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that has already been passed to createRoot()' from components/skins/eidolon-skin.tsx:102. mount() calls createRoot(container) with no guard, so a remount (React StrictMode double-invoke in dev, a skin switch, or any re-entry) creates a second root over the same DOM node — orphaning the first root's Three.js scene and leaking a WebGL context.

RED first: add a test that calls mount() twice on the same container and asserts createRoot is invoked only once (or that the prior root is unmounted first) and that no second root leaks. Confirm it FAILS.

Fix by making mount() idempotent: if this.root already exists for the container, reuse it (root.render) or unmount cleanly before creating a new one; ensure unmount() disposes the Three.js renderer and nulls this.root.
Verify: npm test green (including the new idempotency test), npm run typecheck exits 0.
Verify: npm run test:e2e passes AND the browser console during the smoke run contains no 'already been passed to createRoot' warning.

description: Let a newly-configured brain recover without a full page reload
steps: CONTEXT (found 2026-07-18 while building the no-keys degradation eval): the degradation task asks that enabling a brain restore the send flow WITHOUT a reload. It does not today, and that is a real gap rather than a test artifact: lib/use-capabilities.ts fetches /api/config exactly ONCE on mount (a useEffect keyed only on injected config/fetch), there is no polling and no exposed refresh(), and the no-brain banner itself instructs the user to '…then reload'. Deliberately NOT fixed while writing the eval, because changing app behaviour nobody asked for is out of scope for a test task — filed here so it is an explicit product decision.

DECIDED 2026-07-18 (Felipe): reload-to-recover is ACCEPTED. Setting a server key means redeploying or restarting anyway, so the user is already in a reload-shaped moment; polling every open tab forever to serve a once-per-deployment event is a bad trade. Closed by confirming the banner wording is accurate rather than by implementing hot-recovery. No app change.

DECIDE FIRST (ask Felipe if unsure): is reload-to-recover acceptable UX? If yes, close this task by aligning the docs/banner wording and delete the 'without reload' expectation from the degradation task. If no, implement below.

RED first: extend tests/e2e/degradation.spec.ts with a case that boots against a zero-key /api/config, then re-routes /api/config to report a configured brain, triggers the refresh path, and asserts the no-brain banner disappears and SEND works — all WITHOUT page.reload(). Confirm it FAILS.

Implement a refresh seam on useCapabilities (e.g. a returned refresh() plus an optional focus/visibility-change revalidate), keeping the single-fetch-on-mount default so nothing starts polling the server on a timer.
Update the NO_BRAIN_MESSAGE in lib/capabilities.ts so it no longer tells the user to reload if a reload is no longer required.
If any root app file changed, re-run: node skill/agent-face/scripts/sync-template.mjs
Verify: npm test green and npm run typecheck exits 0.
Verify: the new degradation case passes without any page.reload() call in it.

description: Add component-level test coverage to reach the 80% floor
steps: CONTEXT (measured 2026-07-18, first time coverage was ever run): overall coverage is 58.21% statements / 57.68% branches / 60.95% lines, against a stated 80% floor. The shortfall is NOT spread evenly — business logic is already healthy (lib/settings 100%, lib/providers 88.6%, lib/chat 87.2%, lib/face 86.4%, lib/audio 84.5%, lib/tts 82.2%). It is concentrated in React components sitting at 0%: agent-face.tsx (274 lines), settings-panel.tsx (362), page.tsx (216), face-hud.tsx (108), stt-status.tsx, kokoro-status.tsx, face-skin.tsx, eidolon-skin-view.tsx, layout.tsx.

WHY THEY ARE AT ZERO: no React component testing library is installed. Those components ARE exercised — by the Playwright e2e suite — but @vitest/coverage-v8 cannot see browser-driven execution, so it reports 0%. The number is honest but it understates real verification.

DECIDE FIRST: is unit-testing components the right investment, or is Playwright coverage sufficient for view code? If the latter, the honest move is to EXCLUDE components from the vitest coverage scope and state that in the config, NOT to leave a permanently-red 80% target nobody intends to hit. Ask Felipe.

IF unit-testing: add @testing-library/react + @testing-library/jest-dom, and test behaviour rather than markup — the capability matrix driving button state, settings-panel reconciliation, HUD readouts reflecting emotion, status components reflecting model state.
Do NOT chase the percentage with assertion-free render smoke tests. A test that renders a component and asserts nothing raises coverage while proving nothing, which is worse than the honest 58%.
lib/face-points.ts is 8.95% and is largely a static coordinate table — consider excluding data modules rather than writing tests that assert a table equals itself.
RAISE the thresholds in vitest.config.ts as real coverage improves. Never lower them to make a build pass.
Verify: npm run test:coverage exits 0 and the reported statements percentage has genuinely increased from the recorded 58.21% baseline.
