description: Fix the React Compiler correctness findings surfaced by the first lint run
steps: CONTEXT (2026-07-18, the first time npm run lint ever ran): 24 react-hooks/* errors across five working, shipped, Playwright-covered modules. They are legitimate signals, NOT false positives. To ship a working lint gate without rushing a risky refactor of the render path, they were downgraded to 'warn' in eslint.config.mjs — visibly, with a comment naming this task. They still print on every run.

react-hooks/refs — refs read during render: lib/use-orchestrator.ts:155 (6 occurrences) and components/face-skin.tsx:47. Reading a ref during render is not guaranteed stable under concurrent rendering.

react-hooks/set-state-in-effect — setState called synchronously inside an effect, causing cascading renders: components/settings-panel.tsx:71 and :101, lib/use-capabilities.ts:74 and :84. Note use-capabilities' effects are load-bearing for SSR-safe hydration (see the hydration-mismatch task) — fix WITHOUT reintroducing a server/client render divergence, and re-run tests/e2e/degradation.spec.ts which gates on zero uncaught errors.

react-hooks/purity and react-hooks/immutability — components/agent-face.tsx (impure calls during render at 78/79/229/230/231; local mutation after render at 86/89/93/102/136). This is the 60fps particle loop, so preserve the ref-based no-re-render design while making the render pass itself pure.
react-hooks/use-memo — components/kokoro-status.tsx:37 and components/stt-status.tsx:43 expect an inline function expression as the first argument.
Fix rule-family by rule-family, not all at once, and RESTORE each rule to 'error' in eslint.config.mjs as its family is cleared. Do not leave the downgrade block as permanent furniture.
Verify: npm run lint exits 0 with the fixed rule families set back to 'error'.
Verify: npm test and npx playwright test both stay green — these are behavioural changes to the render path, so a passing lint alone does NOT mean the face still works.
Verify: tests/e2e/degradation.spec.ts still reports zero uncaught page errors (its KNOWN_TRACKED allowlist must stay empty).

description: Make the gates runnable without CI (npm run verify as the single source of truth)
steps: CONTEXT (2026-07-18): the first real CI run failed with 'The job was not started because your account is locked due to a billing issue' — all three jobs died before a single step executed. That exposed a design flaw, not just an outage: the workflow had become the ONLY definition of 'green'. An open-source project whose contributors have no access to the maintainer's Actions account cannot put its gates exclusively in CI.

Create scripts/verify.mjs — plain Node, no dependencies beyond the project's own — running every gate in one place: lint, typecheck, unit tests + coverage, build, skill smoke tests, app-template parity. Support --e2e to add Playwright and --list to print gates without running.

Scrub NODE_ENV for the non-build gates and force NODE_ENV=production for the build gate: an ambient NODE_ENV=development makes next build load React's dev build for part of the SSR graph and prod for the rest, killing prerendering with a null-dispatcher useContext error. That footgun cost this project a day.
Report a per-gate PASS/FAIL summary, print the tail of the output for failures only, and exit non-zero if ANY gate fails. A skipped gate must be reported as SKIP, never folded into a clean green.
Rewrite .github/workflows/ci.yml to simply CALL `npm run verify` rather than re-listing the steps, so local and CI cannot drift. Keep e2e as a separate job only because it needs a browser download.
Document `npm run verify` in CONTRIBUTING.md as the pre-PR command.
Verify: `npm run verify` exits 0 on a clean tree and prints a per-gate summary.
Verify: injecting a deliberate lint violation makes it exit 1 AND name the failing gate.
Verify: `npm run verify -- --e2e` runs the browser suite too and exits 0.
Verify: .github/workflows/ci.yml no longer duplicates the gate list — it calls npm run verify.

description: Make the dev-server port kill work on Linux (not just macOS)
steps: CONTEXT (found 2026-07-18 by running the gates in a Linux container instead of only on macOS): dev.mjs freed the port using `lsof -ti tcp:PORT` and, when lsof was ABSENT, returned [] — silently reporting 'port is free' and skipping the kill entirely. A stock node:22-bookworm image has NONE of lsof, ss, fuser or netstat, so on Linux that was the NORMAL case. The 'always kill any previous dev server' guarantee — a hard project requirement — silently did nothing there.

Replace the single-tool lookup with a strategy chain: lsof (macOS/most Linux) -> pure-Node /proc scan (parse /proc/net/tcp{,6} for the LISTEN inode, then match /proc/<pid>/fd symlinks against socket:[inode]) -> ss/fuser -> netstat on Windows. Keep it PORT-SCOPED; never kill by process name.
When no strategy can inspect the port, WARN explicitly rather than returning [] silently — a quiet wrong answer is worse than a loud unknown.

Fix dev.test.ts's portOwners() helper, which called lsof directly and so had the identical bug: on Linux its own sanity check failed before the real assertion ran. Import findPidsOnPort from dev.mjs so the test exercises the shipped lookup rather than a parallel imitation.
Verify: npm test and npm run test:skill pass on macOS (no regression).
Verify: the full `npm run verify` gate passes inside a Linux container with no lsof installed.
Verify: the /proc path genuinely finds a listener — the decoy-listener test must PASS on Linux, not merely skip.

description: Local Claude Agent SDK bridge (bridge/) exposing an OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions for the claude-code agent-bridge kind
steps: Create a NEW top-level bridge/ directory with its OWN package.json (name claude-agent-bridge, private, type module; deps: @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk; start script runs src/server.mjs). Do NOT add the SDK to the root package.json and do NOT add bridge to COPY_DIRS in skill/agent-face/scripts/sync-template.mjs — the SDK must never enter the deployed app's dependency tree, the client bundle, or the portable skill template.

Write bridge/README.md stating plainly: this is a LOCAL, personal-use bridge that lets the operator's OWN Claude Code agent (their own subscription login) answer the face. Quote Anthropic's Agent SDK docs — 'Unless previously approved, Anthropic does not allow third party developers to offer claude.ai login or rate limits for their products, including agents built on the Claude Agent SDK.' — and state that this bridge is therefore NOT a user-facing auth feature, NOT a hosted service, and NOT a Vercel route; it binds 127.0.0.1 by default and must stay on the operator's machine. Also state the tradeoff: this gives the browser face your actual Claude Code agent with its memory, tools and filesystem access — more powerful and more dangerous than a stateless API brain.

Implement the fail-closed auth guard in bridge/src/env.mjs: refuse to start when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN is set, because the underlying CLI silently prefers a key and switches billing from the Claude subscription to metered API usage; escape hatch CLAUDE_BRIDGE_ALLOW_API_KEY=1. The bridge resolves NO credentials itself — the SDK subprocess self-authenticates via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (claude setup-token) or the ~/.claude credential store. This ports the guard from hermes-agent's claude_agent_sdk_session.py, which exists because a shell with provider keys exported would otherwise silently bill per-token.

Implement bridge/src/server.mjs + bridge/src/openai-http.mjs: POST /v1/chat/completions accepting {model, messages, max_tokens, stream, temperature} (max_tokens and temperature are accepted and ignored — the Agent SDK has no such knobs), optional inbound Authorization: Bearer check against CLAUDE_BRIDGE_TOKEN (maps to AGENT_BRIDGE_KEY app-side), GET /healthz, and a per-process turn lock (a concurrent second request waits briefly, then 429s).

Implement bridge/src/sse.mjs writing OpenAI chat.completion.chunk frames: role chunk first, then choices[0].delta.content chunks, a finish chunk carrying finish_reason and usage, the data: [DONE] terminator, a ': keepalive' SSE comment every 30s of inactivity, and client-disconnect detection that aborts and interrupts the running SDK query (barge-in in the face aborts the fetch).

Implement bridge/src/session.mjs as one-shot query()-per-request with resume: send ONLY the latest user message; pass the client's {role:'system'} text as systemPrompt {type:'preset', preset:'claude_code', append}; capture session_id from the init system message; start FRESH when the replayed history contains no assistant turns; a stale resume HARD-FAILS, so retire the stored id and retry ONCE fresh with a bounded continuity digest built from the replayed history tail (bridge/src/digest.mjs, ~4000 char cap); init timeout 60s, turn timeout 600s.

Subprocess teardown: the SDK owns a Claude Code CLI subprocess that LEAKS if not disposed. Assign the query handle before any await so every exit path — normal result, client disconnect, timeout, thrown error including half-connected startup — reaches one dispose() that aborts, best-effort interrupts, and drains the iterator. This is hermes-agent's hardest-won lesson (three teardown sites, incl. the half-connected case).

Implement bridge/src/projector.mjs: unwrap only stream_event frames whose event.type is content_block_delta and delta.type is text_delta, and DROP any frame carrying parent_tool_use_id (subagent monologue floods the user otherwise). v1 is TEXT ONLY — no OpenAI tool_calls projection: server-side tools (ServerToolUseBlock, e.g. web_search) return their results inside the same assistant message and never a {role:'tool'} echo, so emitting tool_calls would create a dangling tool_call_id that breaks the client's full-history replay (hermes pins this with a dedicated test). The face client only reads delta.content anyway. No MCP configuration in v1 (also sidesteps the SDK-serializes-env-onto-argv leak; if MCP is ever added, use a strict env allowlist).

Keep the real SDK behind bridge/src/sdk.mjs (dynamic import) and inject the query function into session/server, so every unit test runs headless against a scripted fake — the real SDK spawns a CLI subprocess and requires a subscription login, which is exercised only in UAT. Root typecheck must pass without the SDK installed.

Write bridge/test/*.test.ts (vitest, @vitest-environment node): auth-guard fail-closed both vars + escape hatch; SSE contract (role chunk, deltas, finish+usage, [DONE], keepalive comment invisible to eventsource-parser); projector filtering incl. parent_tool_use_id drop and text-only projection; fresh/resume/stale-retry state machine incl. the single digest retry; dispose called on every exit path; and a round-trip test driving the REAL lib/providers/agent-bridge adapter (injected fetch) against an in-process bridge instance with a fake query — proving writer and parser agree without any live agent.
Add '@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk' to the FORBIDDEN list in tests/client-boundary.test.ts (the existing '@anthropic-ai/sdk' entry does not match it under the endsWith check).

Document the wiring in bridge/README.md and skill/agent-face/references/backends.md: AGENT_BRIDGE_KIND=claude-code, AGENT_BRIDGE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8787, optional AGENT_BRIDGE_KEY=CLAUDE_BRIDGE_TOKEN value, in .env.local only.

Verify: npm test passes at the repo root — vitest's existing '**/*.test.ts' include picks up bridge/test/ — and bridge/src is intentionally OUTSIDE the coverage ratchet's include list (headless correctness is gated by these tests, not the ratchet; same principle as the skill CLI scripts).

Verify: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm run build and node skill/agent-face/scripts/sync-template.mjs --check all pass with zero changes to the root package.json or COPY_DIRS, and @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk appears in no package.json except bridge/package.json.

Verify: (UAT, human — NOT headless) with no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN in the shell and a subscription login present, run: cd bridge; npm install; npm start — then set AGENT_BRIDGE_KIND=claude-code and AGENT_BRIDGE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8787 in .env.local and hold a spoken conversation with the face. Confirm continuity across turns, barge-in interrupts with no orphan claude processes left in ps, bridge-restart recovery via the continuity digest, and billing that stays on the subscription (no metered usage appears).

description: Finalize the deliverable prd.json at repo root
steps: PREREQUISITE: DEPENDS ON all prior tasks (this file is the project deliverable).
Ensure root prd.json is a bare JSON array of task objects, each with exactly the keys category, description, steps, passes, headless_verifiable.
Ensure category values are within {backend, frontend, docs, skill, infra} and passes is false for every task until built.
